title: Spinning Gotland wool
subtitle: A semi-lustre Grey breed spun woollen or worsted for a bouncy DK-weight yarn with natural silver-grey sheen
excerpt: Gotland is a Swedish heritage breed with a distinctive silver-grey fleece and semi-lustre handle. The fibre sits between lustre longwool and down breed in character: it can be spun woollen for a lofty yarn or worsted for a smooth one with a natural sheen.
sourceNotes: Gotland breed characteristics are documented by the Swedish Sheep Breeders Association and in UK rare-breed literature. The Gotland is kept in small flocks across the UK and Scandinavia. Spinning behaviour is observation-led from guild notes.

glossaryTerms.definition: A spinning preparation and technique in which carded fibres are spun with a long draw, producing a lofty, airy yarn with more halo and insulation than worsted-spun yarn.
The natural sheen visible in the spun yarn of certain breeds, including Gotland, produced by flat fibre scales that reflect light along the fibre length.
A natural cluster of fibres in the shorn fleece. Gotland staple is typically 8 to 12 cm, with a defined wavy crimp and a silver tip in the purebred.

content.text: Gotland fleece is naturally silver-grey, with a range from pale silver to dark charcoal depending on the individual animal. The
in the fibre is subtle compared with Wensleydale or Bluefaced Leicester but it gives the spun yarn a soft sheen, especially visible in worsted preparations. The

length is 8 to 12 cm with a defined wavy crimp. Gotland has a micron range of approximately 29 to 34 microns: not next-to-skin soft, but comfortable for outerwear, accessories, and colourwork sweaters worn over a base layer. Both
and worsted preparations work; the choice depends on the intended use.

attrs.title: Wheel safety
attrs.body: Keep hair, scarves, and loose cuffs back from the drive band and flyer. The flyer turns fast and catches fabric immediately.

items.name: Gotland raw locks, combed top, or carded roving, 100 g
Spinning wheel, castle or Saxony style
Measuring tape for checking wraps per inch

content.text: yarn: card the raw locks on hand cards into a rolag. The carding introduces air and opens the crimp, producing a lofty preparation that will spin into a bouncy yarn. For a worsted yarn: comb the locks straight on hand combs or a hackle and draw off into top. The combed preparation gives more sheen and a smoother fabric.

Set the wheel to a ratio of 6:1 to 8:1 for a DK-weight single. Gotland's medium staple takes a moderate twist. Spin with a short forward draw for worsted or a long draw for woollen. Gotland singles are stable and resist over-twisting more readily than finer merino or Corriedale; if the singles are twisting back on themselves when dropped, add ply twist to correct rather than reducing singles twist, as Gotland yarn needs more twist to be durable.

Ply two singles in the opposite direction at a ratio of 4:1 to 5:1 to produce a balanced 2-ply DK-weight yarn. Gotland's natural grey colours blend beautifully when plied from two bobbins of slightly different shades taken from different individual animals. Wind the skein on the niddy-noddy, tie loosely at four points, and soak in warm water for 15 minutes. Snap once to set the twist and hang to dry without weight: the woollen-spun version especially is best dried without tension to preserve the loft.

items.problem: The yarn feels coarser than expected and is scratchy
items.cause: The animal's micron count is at the high end for the breed, or the fleece came from an older animal with coarser secondary fibres
items.fix: Use Gotland for outerwear or bags rather than next-to-skin garments. Blending with 20 to 30 percent Bluefaced Leicester top will soften the handle while preserving the grey colour.
